name: Sticky Bottom Banner CTA
description: A fixed-bottom sticky banner CTA that slides in after a scroll threshold and offers a one-line nudge with a primary action and optional secondary link. Includes a dismiss control and four tone variants.

descriptions.descriptive: A pill-shaped banner that slides up from the bottom of the viewport once the visitor passes a configurable scroll depth. One-line headline, optional inline description on desktop, primary CTA, optional secondary text link, and an X dismiss control. Four tones — neutral, muted, inverse, and primary — and respects the site's CtaButton variant via styleKit.
descriptions.usage: Use as a persistent low-friction reminder on long marketing pages — newsletter signup, free-trial start, demo booking. Pair with a longer in-flow CTA section (CtaEditorialSplit, CtaCountdown) so the sticky banner is the safety net rather than the primary ask.
descriptions.audienceFit: Editorial sites with long scrolls, B2B SaaS marketing pages, content-heavy publishers running newsletter signups, and any site whose conversions skew below the fold.
